title: centaurea attica subsp. asperula
intro: Centaurea attica subsp. asperula, also known as Attica knapweed or asperula, is a plant species belonging to the aster family and is native to Greece. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. Centaurea attica subsp. asperula can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
